summary: Iteration 3 (real brand deals + OTP email verification + optional-image verification + student card PNG download + premium hero) fully validated. 11/11 backend pytest cases pass. 6/6 critical frontend flows pass in Playwright: hero renders with 'cheat code' h1 + stats + 3 brand chips + CTAs, signup redirects to /verify-email, wrong OTP shows 'Invalid code', correct OTP verifies + redirects to /dashboard, /verify submits without images and auto-approves, /card exposes card-download-btn, brand offer detail button reads 'Claim & continue to brand'.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1380 lines — approaching the 700-line split threshold; consider splitting auth, offers, outlets, coupons, scan into separate routers.
OTP: no attempt-limit / brute-force protection on /auth/verify-otp — anyone knowing an email can try 10^6 codes. Consider counting failed attempts per OTP and invalidating after 5.
OTP resend: throttle is per-OTP `created_at`; if user requests via /auth/send-otp AFTER a used verify, they'll be blocked because the last row is still recent. Minor UX nit.
Verification is auto-approved regardless of image content — acceptable for MVP but should be flagged for prod.
login route (/api/auth/login) has no lockout on repeated failures — required by playbook but out of iter-3 scope.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@savycampusdeals.in / Admin@123. Fresh students created dynamically per test; OTP fetched via `mongosh mongodb://localhost:27017 --quiet --eval "db.getSiblingDB('savycampusdeals').otp_codes.findOne({email:'<email>'}).otp"`
seed_data_creation: None — startup seed handles 14 real brand offers + 6 outlets. Test users are TEST_-prefixed and cleaned up in teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 3 shipped cleanly. OTP flow: register writes to db.otp_codes; verify-otp requires exact 6-digit code; send-otp throttled at 60s. Admin role bypasses email OTP gate via get_verified_user. Backend test file /app/backend/tests/test_iteration3.py can be reused. Outlet offer detail page: outlet offers are claimed inline from /outlets/{id} (no /offers/{id} navigation), so a UI test for outlet 'Claim coupon' text needs to navigate the offer card link directly (they render but are inline).
